Title: Classical approaches to UGT functional analysis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suggested to be linked to callose synthesis, but precise function remains obscure
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein-protein interaction analysis; GFP subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Protein-protein interaction analysis suggested a link between Arabidopsis UGT75B1 and callose synthesis. 2. The protein is associated with the callose synthase complex. 3. Its precise physiological role remains unclear.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is explicitly discussed, but its exact nutrient-metabolism relevance is weak and its function is unresolved.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: callose synthase complex components (unnamed in excerpt)

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase (plant secondary product glycosyltransferase, PSPG box-containing)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: glycosylates flavonoid substrate(s) involved in pigment formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification/accumulation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: confers dark pigmentation of maize grains; Bronze1 mutation affects flavonoid glycosylation-linked pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bronze1 was the first plant UGT-encoding gene identified. 2. It was discovered through genetic analysis of maize grain pigmentation. 3. It later proved to encode a flavonoid UGT linked to pigment accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a historical example in a review; in vivo details are limited in this excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for manipulating grain pigment/flavonoid traits in maize, but specific engineering guidance is not provided in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: glycosylates the cyanohydrin intermediate in dhurrin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: involved in synthesis of the cyanogenic glucoside dhurrin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GFP fusion protein colocalization; protein complex/pathway association
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sorghum UGT85B1 was shown to participate in dhurrin synthesis. 2. GFP colocalization supported its involvement in a multienzymatic complex. 3. This linked enzyme localization and protein organization to cyanogenic glucoside production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The excerpt emphasizes complex formation rather than full biochemical details.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to engineer cyanogenic defense chemistry, with caution because cyanogenic compounds affect plant defense and toxicity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin-related defense and antioxidant detoxification during hypersensitive response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates scopoletin levels; downregulation increases oxidative stress and indicates contribution of scopoletin to active oxygen detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gain- and loss-of-function in transgenic plants; metabolite/phenotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gain- and loss-of-function analysis showed TOGT regulates scopoletin levels in tobacco. 2. Scopoletin accumulates strongly during the hypersensitive response against tobacco mosaic virus. 3. Downregulated TOGT plants showed increased oxidative stress, linking this pathway to detoxification of active oxygen species.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review frames TOGT as one of the few UGTs with strong in vivo functional demonstration.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for scopoletin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: tobacco mosaic virus; hypersensitive response; oxidative burst
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: downregulation associated with increased oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering disease-responsive phenylpropanoid defense metabolism and oxidative stress buffering in tobacco-like systems.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers glucose to the 5-OH position of anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for 5-O-glucosylation of anthocyanins in planta; mutant anthocyanins lack a glucose moiety at the 5-OH position
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray coexpression analysis; metabolite profiling; recombinant enzyme assay; T-DNA mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ugt75c1 was identified as upregulated in PAP1-overproducing plants and co-regulated with anthocyanin pathway genes. 2. Recombinant UGT75C1 glycosylated anthocyanidins/flavonols in vitro. 3. T-DNA mutants lacked the 5-O-glucose on anthocyanins, demonstrating that UGT75C1 is an anthocyanin 5-O-glycosyltransferase in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is one of the clearest examples in the review of combining transcriptomics and metabolomics to define UGT function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: co-regulated with anthocyanin biosynthetic genes during development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated in PAP1-overproducing plants; co-regulated with anthocyanin pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PAP1 (regulatory association/coexpression, not direct physical interaction established in excerpt)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PAP1 overproduction caused only small broader metabolome changes aside from anthocyanin/flavonol overaccumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tailoring anthocyanin glycosylation patterns and pigment quality in metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray analysis; targeted metabolite profiling; in vitro enzymatic characterization (as summarized in review/table)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Medicago UGTs were identified by combining microarray and targeted metabolite profiling. 2. Ugt73k1 was among the UGTs implicated in triterpene saponin synthesis. 3. This illustrates application of functional genomics beyond Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic position/substrate not detailed in the excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering saponin profiles for defense or phytochemical applications.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray analysis; targeted metabolite profiling; in vitro enzymatic characterization (as summarized in review/table)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ugt71g1 was identified among Medicago UGTs involved in triterpene saponin synthesis. 2. Its discovery relied on combining transcript and metabolite datasets. 3. The study highlighted functional genomics as a route to define UGT roles in specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic position/substrate not detailed in the excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering saponin profiles for defense or phytochemical applications.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Ugt75c1; genes encoding enzymes of the anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates/upregulates Ugt75c1 and anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulator involved in flavonoid metabolism; overproduction strongly elevates anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overproduction causes marked overaccumulation of anthocyanins and some flavonol derivatives
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; genome-wide microarray survey; non-targeted metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 overproduction upregulated Ugt75c1 in a genome-wide microarray survey. 2. PAP1 is a MYB factor involved in regulation of flavonoid metabolism. 3. PAP1 overproduction caused strong anthocyanin accumulation with limited broader metabolome perturbation except some flavonol derivatives.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: developmental cues (inferred from co-regulation across development); specific upstream signals not stated in excerpt
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: linked to developmental anthocyanin program; assessed in overproducing plants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: only small metabolome changes beyond anthocyanin and some flavonol derivative overaccumulation were reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A valuable regulatory lever for boosting anthocyanin content, though it may need pairing with tailoring enzymes like UGT75C1 to control product forms precisely.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This regulator is discussed as context for discovering UGT75C1 in the review.
